evals.name: health_check_request
evals.prompt: Is this codebase healthy? Give me a repo health check before we onboard two new developers.
evals.expected_output: A one-page health snapshot: structure/stack map, risk hotspots backed by git churn evidence (actual commands run), dependency and security posture routed to specialists, test/CI state, and a prioritised action list with a routing receipt line.
assertions: Ran (or attempted) the git churn commands rather than guessing hotspots
Every hotspot cites concrete evidence: churn count, size, or missing test path
Routed dependency and security depth to specialists instead of re-deriving
Produced a prioritised action list, not an unranked dump
Included the routing receipt (which specialists ran or why not)

evals.name: shallow_git_honesty
evals.prompt: Health check this repo please. (It was just imported — git history is a single squashed commit.)
evals.expected_output: States that churn analysis is impossible with one commit, grades Risk Hotspots as insufficient data, and grounds the rest of the snapshot in what is verifiable (structure, tests, dependencies).
assertions: Did not fabricate churn-based hotspots from a single-commit history
Explicitly marked hotspot analysis as insufficient data
Still delivered the verifiable sections (structure, test state, dependencies)

evals.name: should_not_trigger_cleanup
evals.prompt: Clean up this repo — fix the naming inconsistencies and stale config.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT produce a health snapshot. Drift-fixing requests route to project-maintainer.
assertions: Did not produce a graded health snapshot
Routed to or applied project-maintainer (drift detection and minimal fixes)
